metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4820
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Noelin1 interacts with the AMPAR with high affinity (Figure 1,2), co-localizes with APMAR at the PSD in cultured hippocampal neurons (Figure 4), but does not affect AMPAR rise- and decay time nor desensitization properties (Figure 3).

Figure 5: chemical LTP induced in DIV21 primary neurons resulting in increased levels of synaptic AMPAR (surface GluA2/GRIA2) and Noelin1. Authors conclude; "Noelin1 expression is regulated in an activity-dependent manner and, therefore, might have a role in the accumulation and stabilization of synaptic AMPA receptors at the PSD."

Figure 6, S6, 7: Single-particle tracking shows that Noelin1 reduces lateral mobility of both synaptic and extra-synaptic GluA1-containing receptors and affects short-term plasticity. While the ECM does not constrain the synaptic pool of AMPARs and acts only extrasynaptically, Noelin1 contributes to synaptic potentiation by limiting AMPAR mobility at synaptic sites.. Experimental description: M&M for Figure 6 refer to Klueva et al. 2014 (PMID: 25225098), which used rats.

antibody:
Noelin1, Neuromab, Clone K96/7
